title: Request normalization
implementation_summary: Added deterministic metadata-only Permission And Audit Binding request normalization for RP00.P02.M05.S02.

The S02 policy, normalizer module, getter, validator assertions, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README entry, RP00 validator checks, and service tests bind a frozen request-normalization surface after RP00.P02.M05.S01 and RP00.P02.M04.S14.
The normalizer requires the S01 service entrypoint contract status and the S14 Claude review required-routing closeout status to be production_ready before any metadata request can be accepted.

The normalized request carries tenant, actor, resource tenant, nullable Matter, permission decision, audit event, role-check, deny-rule, security-trim, binding context, required evidence, and acceptance gate metadata.

The implementation fails closed on wrong entrypoint, wrong source microphase, non-production-ready dependencies, missing or untrimmed required refs, binding context drift, required evidence drift, acceptance gate drift, forbidden runtime/write/bypass claims, unknown claims, and invalid claim inputs.

The allowed scope remains metadata-only and does not execute service logic, create runtime routes, evaluate runtime permissions, call AuthZ evaluators, run permission engines, apply security trimming, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write database, write storage, persist product state, mutate entity registries, or replace human approval.
Claude Opus 4.8 max read-only review returned GO with no P0, P1, P2, or required fixes. Two informational advisories were adjudicated as non-blocking.
RP00.P02.M05 remains open and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S03 Tenant boundary precheck. RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
